wikipedia_title: Hearing the shape of a drum
display_title: Hearing the shape of a drum

annotations.label: Hearing the shape problem

anchor.snippet: the conceptual problem of " hearing the shape of a drum " refers to the prospect of inferring information

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib declaration formalizing Kac's inverse spectral problem of hearing the shape of a drum.

annotations.label: Frequencies depend on shape

anchor.snippet: The frequencies at which a drumhead can vibrate depend on its shape

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib formalization of drumhead vibration frequencies depending on planar shape.

annotations.label: Helmholtz equation frequencies

anchor.snippet: The Helmholtz equation calculates the frequencies if the shape is known

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the Laplacian on finite-dimensional real inner product spaces, but I found no Helmholtz-equation frequency theorem for drum domains.

annotations.label: Frequencies as Laplacian eigenvalues

anchor.snippet: These frequencies are the eigenvalues of the Laplacian in the space

annotations.note: Mathlib has general eigenspaces and Laplacian definitions, but no theorem identifying physical drum frequencies with Laplacian eigenvalues.

annotations.label: Central inverse question

anchor.snippet: A central question is whether the shape can be predicted if the frequencies are known

annotations.note: I found no declaration stating the inverse problem of recovering a shape from its frequency spectrum.

annotations.label: Reuleaux triangle example

anchor.snippet: whether a Reuleaux triangle can be recognized in this way

annotations.note: I found no Reuleaux-triangle inverse-spectral example or Reuleaux-triangle declaration in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Negative answer to Kac question

anchor.snippet: the frequencies determine the shape was finally answered in the negative

annotations.note: I found no formal statement of the negative answer to Kac's question in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Clamped membrane model

anchor.snippet: the drum is conceived as an elastic membrane whose boundary is clamped

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib model of an elastic membrane with clamped boundary conditions.

annotations.label: Drum domain

anchor.snippet: represented as a domain D in the plane

annotations.note: I found no dedicated declaration for a drum domain as a planar domain in the inverse-spectral setting.

annotations.label: Dirichlet eigenvalues

anchor.snippet: Denote by λ n the Dirichlet eigenvalues for D

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes eigenvalues of linear endomorphisms, but I found no Dirichlet Laplacian eigenvalue sequence for a domain.

annotations.label: Isospectral domains

anchor.snippet: Two domains are said to be isospectral (or homophonic) if they have the same eigenvalues

annotations.note: Mathlib defines spectra of algebra elements, but I found no isospectral-domain relation for Laplacian eigenvalues of planar domains.

annotations.label: Eigenvalues as tones

anchor.snippet: the Dirichlet eigenvalues are precisely the fundamental tones

annotations.note: Mathlib has eigenvalue infrastructure, but I found no formalization relating Dirichlet eigenvalues to fundamental tones.

annotations.label: Fourier coefficient occurrence

anchor.snippet: they appear naturally as Fourier coefficients

annotations.note: Mathlib defines Fourier coefficients on the additive circle, but I found no theorem that drum Dirichlet eigenvalues appear as such coefficients.

annotations.label: Inverse spectral formulation

anchor.snippet: what can be inferred on D if one knows only the values of λ n

annotations.note: I found no declaration formalizing the inverse spectral question for a domain from the values of its Dirichlet eigenvalues.

annotations.label: Isospectral nonuniqueness question

anchor.snippet: are there two distinct domains that are isospectral

annotations.note: I found no formalized existence question or theorem about distinct isospectral planar domains.

annotations.label: Higher-dimensional related problems

anchor.snippet: Dirichlet problem for the Laplacian on domains in higher dimensions or on Riemannian manifolds

annotations.note: Mathlib has Riemannian manifolds and Euclidean Laplacian infrastructure, but I found no Dirichlet spectral problem for higher-dimensional domains or manifolds.

annotations.label: Other elliptic operators

anchor.snippet: Cauchy–Riemann operator or Dirac operator

annotations.note: I found no Cauchy-Riemann or Dirac elliptic-operator declarations corresponding to this example.

annotations.label: Neumann boundary condition

anchor.snippet: the Neumann boundary condition

annotations.note: I found no Neumann boundary-condition formalization for PDE eigenvalue problems in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Milnor isospectral tori

anchor.snippet: the existence of a pair of 16-dimensional flat tori

annotations.note: I found no formalization of Milnor's pair of 16-dimensional isospectral flat tori.

annotations.label: Gordon-Webb-Wolpert domains

anchor.snippet: a pair of regions in the plane that have different shapes but identical eigenvalues

annotations.note: I found no declaration for the Gordon-Webb-Wolpert noncongruent isospectral planar domains.

annotations.label: Concave polygon regions

anchor.snippet: The regions are concave polygons

annotations.note: Mathlib defines general polygons, but I found no concave-polygon classification of the Gordon-Webb-Wolpert regions.

annotations.label: Similar isospectral examples

anchor.snippet: constructed numerous similar examples

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib declarations for families of planar isospectral examples similar to Gordon-Webb-Wolpert.

annotations.label: Inaudibility for many shapes

anchor.snippet: for many shapes, one cannot hear the shape of the drum completely

annotations.note: I found no theorem in Mathlib stating non-uniqueness of shape from spectrum for many drums.

annotations.label: Partial spectral information

anchor.snippet: some information can be inferred

annotations.note: I found no formal statement of geometric information inferable from the Dirichlet spectrum of a drum domain.

annotations.label: Zelditch positive result

anchor.snippet: the answer to Kac's question is positive if one imposes restrictions to certain convex planar regions with analytic boundary

annotations.note: I found no formalization of Zelditch's positive inverse-spectral result for restricted convex analytic planar domains.

annotations.label: Non-convex analytic open problem

anchor.snippet: two non-convex analytic domains can have the same eigenvalues

annotations.note: I found no Mathlib statement of the open problem about isospectral non-convex analytic domains.

annotations.label: Compact isospectral domains

anchor.snippet: the set of domains isospectral with a given one is compact

annotations.note: I found no theorem that the class of domains isospectral to a fixed domain is compact.

annotations.label: Sphere spectral rigidity

anchor.snippet: the sphere (for instance) is spectrally rigid

annotations.note: I found no spectral-rigidity theorem for spheres in Mathlib.

annotations.label: No continuous isospectral flow

anchor.snippet: the moduli space of Riemann surfaces of a given genus does not admit a continuous isospectral flow

annotations.note: I found no formalization of a no-continuous-isospectral-flow theorem for moduli spaces of Riemann surfaces.

annotations.label: Compact moduli space

anchor.snippet: is compact in the Fréchet–Schwartz topology

annotations.note: I found no declaration for the relevant moduli space or its compactness in the Frechet-Schwartz topology.

annotations.label: Weyl area formula

anchor.snippet: Weyl's formula states that one can infer the area A of the drum

annotations.note: I found no Weyl eigenvalue-counting asymptotic that recovers the area of a drum domain.

annotations.label: Eigenvalue counting function

anchor.snippet: N ( R ) to be the number of eigenvalues smaller than R

annotations.note: I found no eigenvalue-counting function for spectra of Dirichlet Laplacians in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dimension parameter

anchor.snippet: d is the dimension

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes finite dimension as the natural number `Module.finrank`, though not specifically as the parameter in Weyl's formula.

annotations.label: Unit ball volume

anchor.snippet: volume of the d -dimensional unit ball

annotations.note: `EuclideanSpace.volume_ball` gives the volume formula for Euclidean balls, including the d-dimensional unit ball as a special invocation.

annotations.label: Weyl perimeter conjecture

anchor.snippet: the next term in the approximation below would give the perimeter of D

annotations.note: I found no statement of Weyl's perimeter-term conjecture for drum spectra.

annotations.label: Perimeter length notation

anchor.snippet: L denotes the length of the perimeter

annotations.note: I found no perimeter-length notation or perimeter functional for planar drum domains tied to this formula.

annotations.label: Weyl two-term asymptotic

anchor.snippet: then one should have

annotations.note: I found no two-term Weyl asymptotic for the Dirichlet eigenvalue counting function in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ivrii smooth-boundary theorem

anchor.snippet: For a smooth boundary, this was proved

annotations.note: I found no formalization of Ivrii's smooth-boundary two-term Weyl theorem.

annotations.label: Periodic geodesic exclusion

anchor.snippet: not allowed to have a two-parameter family of periodic geodesics

annotations.note: I found no theorem excluding two-parameter families of periodic geodesics for Weyl asymptotics.

annotations.label: Sphere periodic geodesics

anchor.snippet: such as a sphere would have

annotations.note: I found no formal statement that spheres have the relevant two-parameter family of periodic geodesics.

annotations.label: Weyl-Berry correction

anchor.snippet: the correction should be of the order of

annotations.note: I found no statement of the Weyl-Berry spectral correction order in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Boundary Hausdorff dimension

anchor.snippet: D is the Hausdorff dimension of the boundary

annotations.note: Mathlib defines Hausdorff dimension of arbitrary metric-space sets via `dimH`, but I found no Weyl-Berry boundary-dimension parameterization.

annotations.label: Brossard-Carmona disproof

anchor.snippet: This was disproved by J. Brossard and R. A. Carmona

annotations.note: I found no formalization of the Brossard-Carmona disproof of the Weyl-Berry conjecture.

annotations.label: Upper box dimension replacement

anchor.snippet: replace the Hausdorff dimension with the upper box dimension

annotations.note: I found no upper box dimension replacement statement, and no upper box dimension declaration, in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Planar dimension-one case

anchor.snippet: this was proved if the boundary has dimension 1

annotations.note: I found no theorem proving the Weyl-Berry replacement in the planar boundary-dimension-one case.

annotations.label: Higher-dimensional disproof

anchor.snippet: mostly disproved for higher dimensions

annotations.note: I found no higher-dimensional disproof theorem for the Weyl-Berry conjectural correction in Mathlib.
